hub_subtitle: Business English para seguros, reuniões e entrevistas — e o desbloqueio da fala, do primeiro dia ao último

lesson.menu_title: What Did You Do? -- The Past Simple and the Story of Your Career
lesson.menu_desc: A aula em que a Paula, pela primeira vez, é o sujeito da própria história. Por doze aulas a carreira dela aconteceu com ela — a diretora a levou pra Marsh, a cláusula fechou a porta, a Zurich apareceu. Hoje ela conta essa história — e no inglês do passado ela está no começo de cada frase: I began, I got the job, I became a specialist, I left, I went, I made the move. A gramática prova a virada emocional: quem começa a frase é quem age. Ela leva seis verbos irregulares de altíssima relevância (began, got, became, went, made, left) e a forma afirmativa do past simple — regular (-ed) e irregular. A aula é de LEITURA: ela lê a própria história de Gallagher a Zurich, o Greg conta o primeiro grande erro dele, o David lembra que a carreira dele não “só aconteceu”, e a Sofia fecha com a frase que prepara a entrevista da aula 15: “na entrevista, comece cada frase com você.” -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 14 -- What Did You Do?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Paula Morales | Lesson 14 -- What Did You Do? (Past Simple)
lesson.grammar_point: past simple affirmative for finished past events -- regular verbs with -ed (worked, started, joined) and high-frequency irregular verbs (began, got, became, went, made, left)

listenings.text: Hi, Paula. David here. Before your lesson today, I want to tell you a story. It is my story, and it is short. Twenty years ago, I began in a tiny office in Ohio. I got a job as a junior clerk, and I made photocopies all day. I did not speak in meetings, because I was afraid. Then one day, a manager asked me a question in front of everyone, and I answered. It was small, but something changed. After that, I worked hard, I learned the business, and slowly I became a broker. A few years later, I left Ohio, I went to Miami, and last year I became a director. Here is the thing, Paula. For a long time, I thought my career just happened to me. Good luck, good timing, good people. But it did not just happen. I showed up. I answered the question. I made the moves. And today it is your turn. Tell your story, the real one, from your first office to Zurich. You did the work. Now say it out loud.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here. Today you learned the past tense, and I want to say something about it, because it is bigger than grammar. For a year, I watched you tell your story in pieces, and you always said it the same way. They took me to Marsh. The clause closed the door. Zurich called me. Everything happened to you. Someone else always decided. But listen to the sentences you made in this lesson. I began. I got the job. I became a specialist. I left Gallagher. I went to Marsh. I made the move to Zurich. Do you hear it, Paula? In every sentence, you are the subject. You are the one who acts. That is not only grammar. That is the truth. Other people opened doors, yes, but you walked through them, and you did the hard work behind every one. Next Monday you have the Zurich interview, and they are going to ask you one question. What did you do? Do not give them a list of things that happened to you. Give them your story, in your own voice, with you at the start of every sentence. Because that is exactly where you belong. See you Monday, Paula.

reading.rtitle: From Gallagher to Zurich — In My Own Words
paras: I began my career in insurance many years ago. At first, I worked at a small office in São Paulo. I started as an assistant, and I made coffee and photocopies all day. I was young, and I did not speak one word of English.

Then I got a job at Gallagher. Gallagher is a big broker, and I stayed there for years. I worked hard, and I learned everything about large risks — factories, ships, big accounts. I became a specialist. People started to call me with the difficult questions.

One day, a director saw my work. She left Gallagher for Marsh, and she took me with her. So I left Gallagher too, and I went to Marsh. I did not choose it — it happened. Then a contract clause changed everything, and I moved again. For a long time, other people made the big decisions, and I only said yes.

Last year, Zurich called. I made the move, and I joined the commercial team for large risks. This time, the decision was mine. I began at a small office with no English, and today I take calls with Miami. That is my story — and for the first time, I am the one who tells it.
reading.source: Paula Morales — career story, written for the Zurich international desk

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of Paula's story?
choices: It is a list of the insurance products that Zurich sells to big factories.
It is Paula's career story, from a small office to Zurich. For years other people decided for her, but the move to Zurich was her own choice — and now she is the one who tells the story.
It is a note that says Paula never learned English and never wants to.

items: Paula began her career at Zurich.
f
No. She began at a small office in São Paulo as an assistant. Then Gallagher, then Marsh, and Zurich came last, only last year.
At Gallagher, Paula became a specialist in large risks.
t
Yes: "I worked hard... I became a specialist. People started to call me with the difficult questions."
Paula left Gallagher and went to Marsh with a director.
t
Yes: "a director... left Gallagher for Marsh, and she took me with her. So I left Gallagher too, and I went to Marsh."
For years, Paula made all the big decisions in her career herself.
f
The opposite: "For a long time, other people made the big decisions, and I only said yes." That is the point the story turns on.
The move to Zurich was Paula's own decision.
t
Yes: "I made the move... This time, the decision was mine." It is the first choice that was truly hers.

bank.label: Telling your career story / Contar a sua história
items: I began my career many years ago.
I got a job at Gallagher.
I became a specialist in large risks.
I left Gallagher and went to Marsh.
Last year, I made the move to Zurich.

bank.label: Time markers for the past / Marcadores de tempo do passado
items: At first, I worked at a small office.
Then I got a better job.
After that, everything changed.
For years, I stayed at Gallagher.
Last year, Zurich called.

items.situation: Tell me about your first job. Where did you start, and what did you do? Use the past.
tips: I began my career at a small office in São Paulo.
At first, I worked as an assistant, and I made coffee all day.

items.situation: A colleague asks how you learned about large risks. Answer with 'became'.
tips: I worked at Gallagher for years, and I became a specialist.
I learned everything at Gallagher, and I became a specialist in large risks.

items.situation: Greg asks why you left Gallagher. Tell him, using 'left' and 'went'.
tips: A director took me, so I left Gallagher and went to Marsh.
I left Gallagher, and I went to Marsh with my director.

items.situation: David asks when you came to Zurich. Answer with a time marker.
tips: Last year, I made the move to Zurich.
I joined Zurich last year, on the large-risk team.

items.situation: Sofia asks about the best decision in your career. Use 'made'.
tips: I made the move to Zurich, and this time the decision was mine.
The best decision was Zurich -- I made it myself.

items.situation: In one breath: tell your whole story, from your first job to today.
tips: I began at a small office, I became a specialist, and last year I joined Zurich.
I started in São Paulo, I worked at Gallagher, and I made the move to Zurich.
